Cell.description: A C. elegans cell (neuron or muscle, or other non-neuronal cell carried by neuron-graph), keyed by its neuron-graph name and grounded in a WBBT anatomy term. Carries the neuron-graph-native attributes so the viz JSON projection is lossless.

anatomy.description: The WBBT anatomy term this cell is grounded in.

cell_class.description: The neuron-graph cell class (e.g. "ADA" for ADAL/ADAR).

embryonic.description: Whether the cell is born embryonically.

id.description: The neuron-graph cell name, used as the stable key (e.g. "ADAL").

in_head.description: Whether the cell is in the head.

in_tail.description: Whether the cell is in the tail.

nemanode_type.description: Raw NemaNode cell-type code (e.g. "i", "s", "sm", "b"). Retained verbatim for the lossless viz projection; the curated CellType is in `cell_type`.

sexes.description: The sex(es) in which this cell is present, derived from the datasets that observe it. A shared cell has both; sex-specific cells have one — e.g. HSN/VC (hermaphrodite), the male ray neurons R1-R9, CEM, HOA/HOB, SPC, PCA/PCB/PCC, PDC (male). Class-level placeholder cells (``unspecified`` = true) carry no sexes: they stand in for unresolved individual cells, so asserting a sex-presence from dataset membership would be spurious (e.g. "VAn" appears only in a hermaphrodite dataset, but VA-class neurons are not hermaphrodite-specific).

unspecified.description: True when this cell is a class- or group-level placeholder minted for a connection endpoint that the source did not resolve to a specific individual cell — e.g. "VAn" (an unspecified VA-class motor neuron) or a pharyngeal muscle class such as "pm3". Such nodes are not individual cells and carry no sex-presence (empty ``sexes``), so per-cell analyses (sex-specificity, cell counts) can exclude them.

Cell.title: Cell

Connection.description: A reified, n-ary connection between two cells observed in one dataset. Reified (not a plain triple) because it carries type, weight, dataset, and evidence.

dataset.description: The dataset in which this was observed.

evidence.description: Provenance records supporting this connection (open-world, additive).

weight.description: Aggregated connection weight as reported by the observing dataset.

Connection.title: Connection

Connectome.description: Top-level container holding all cells, connections, and datasets of one build. Serves as the tree root for data files and validation.
Connectome.title: Connectome

Dataset.description: A specimen/dataset that observes connections (an EM reconstruction or functional-imaging dataset), from neuron-graph (White 1986, Witvliet 2020, Randi funconn) or Cook et al. 2019.

life_stage.description: The developmental stage of the specimen(s) the dataset observed. Dauer is the stress-induced alternative third larval stage (a hermaphrodite), distinct from the continuous L1->L2->L3->L4->adult progression.

sex.description: A C. elegans biological sex — the sex of the specimen(s) a dataset observed, or the sex in which a sex-qualified assertion (e.g. a neurotransmitter assignment) applies.

strain.description: The C. elegans strain/genotype of the specimen, if known.

Dataset.title: Dataset

Evidence.description: Provenance for a connection. Designed to be additive: v1 records an aggregated weight observed in a dataset; later releases can add individual synaptic-contact detail (section, volume, image) by adding optional slots, without schema churn.

dataset.description: The dataset in which this was observed.

evidence_type.description: What kind of evidence this record represents.

source.description: Pointer to the source data (file, URL, or upstream identifier).

value.description: The evidence payload as text (e.g. the aggregated weight value).

Evidence.title: Evidence

ExpressionConfidence.description: The source's expression mark. Cook 2020 SI6 uses uppercase "X" and lowercase "x"; the distinction is not defined in the source, so lowercase is taken as lower-confidence.
ExpressionConfidence.title: ExpressionConfidence

Gene.description: A C. elegans gene, keyed by its persistent WormBase gene id (WB:WBGene…) as resolved via the Alliance of Genome Resources. Carries the gene symbol, WormBase systematic name, and a functional category. Genes are shared across gene-expression datasets.

category.description: The functional category of the gene, as grouped by the source dataset.

id.description: The persistent WormBase gene id as a curie (e.g. "WB:WBGene00001517").

Gene.title: Gene

GeneCategory.description: Functional category a gene is grouped under in an expression dataset.
GeneCategory.title: GeneCategory

GeneExpression.description: A reified assertion that a cell expresses a gene, compiled in one dataset. Reified (not a plain triple) because it carries the source dataset, evidence, an optional transcript-isoform qualifier (when the source resolves expression to an isoform), and a confidence mark.

cell.description: The cell in which the gene is expressed.

confidence.description: The source's expression mark for this assertion.

dataset.description: The dataset in which this was observed.

evidence.description: Provenance records supporting this connection (open-world, additive).

isoform.description: Transcript-isoform qualifier from the source (e.g. "a", "b") when it distinguishes isoforms; expression stays keyed to the persistent gene. Absent when the source is gene-level.

GeneExpression.title: GeneExpression

LifeStage.description: The developmental stage of a C. elegans specimen.
LifeStage.title: LifeStage

NeurotransmitterAssignment.description: A reified, sex-qualified assertion that a cell uses a neurotransmitter, from the Wang et al. 2024 atlas (eLife 95402). Reified because C. elegans neurotransmitter identity is sexually dimorphic: a male-specific neuron has its own call, and a sex-shared neuron can use different transmitters in the male vs the hermaphrodite. The neurotransmitter code follows the same scheme as ``Cell.neurotransmitter`` (a=ACh, l=Glu, g=GABA, d=dopamine, s=serotonin, u=unknown; letters combine for co-transmission). An assignment is recorded where the call is male-specific or differs between the sexes; the unqualified ``Cell.neurotransmitter`` remains the hermaphrodite / neuron-graph call.

cell.description: The cell in which the gene is expressed.

confidence.description: The source's expression mark for this assertion.

sex.description: A C. elegans biological sex — the sex of the specimen(s) a dataset observed, or the sex in which a sex-qualified assertion (e.g. a neurotransmitter assignment) applies.

source.description: Pointer to the source data (file, URL, or upstream identifier).

NeurotransmitterAssignment.title: NeurotransmitterAssignment

Sex.description: The biological sex of a C. elegans specimen.
Sex.title: Sex

description: Top-level container holding all cells, connections, and datasets of one build. Serves as the tree root for data files and validation.
title: celegans-connectome
